textPlain: The article missed the chance to include the quote from that standard compendium of information and wisdom, The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy:> Since every piece of matter in the Universe is in some way affected by every other piece of matter in the Universe, it is in theory possible to extrapolate the whole of creation — every sun, every planet, their orbits, their composition and their economic and social history from, say, one small piece of fairy cake.
Related. Others?Everything Is Correlated - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=19797844 - May 2019 (53 comments)
If two things e.g. both change over time, they will be correlated. I think it can be good to keep this article in mind
It's just as I suspected - there are NO coincidences.
there is but a single unfolding, and everything is part of it
